reason: Vue 3 frontend conventions and pre-development index for Git tab changes.
GitTab UI changes must follow existing component composition and compact tool-surface patterns.
New Git actions need Pinia store methods and snapshot refresh behavior.
Bridge and store interfaces require typed contracts for Git write operations.
Implementation should avoid forbidden frontend patterns and preserve project quality bar.
The project has no backend; preload bridge changes must respect current frontend-only runtime boundaries.
Git CLI write failures and unavailable fallback paths need clear UI-state error handling.
Bridge command execution should remain constrained, non-shell-injection-prone, and scoped.
Reuse existing Git and AI helpers rather than duplicating command/prompt plumbing.
Feature crosses preload bridge, shared types, store, and Vue component boundaries.
